description: An automation that writes the bedroom lights directly bypasses the single sanctioned writer (script.bedroom_lights_set) and must be flagged.
input:
Review this Home Assistant automation:
```yaml
# ansible/roles/containers/home-assistant/files/automations/bedroom_evening_dim.yaml
- id: bedroom_evening_dim
  alias: Bedroom evening dim
  trigger:
    - platform: sun
      event: sunset
  action:
    - service: light.turn_on
      target:
        entity_id: light.bedroom_lamp_left
      data:
        brightness_pct: 40
```
Repo convention (enforced by validate-ha-config against sanctioned_writers.yml): the bedroom light actuators have exactly ONE sanctioned writer — script.bedroom_lights_set. Every automation that changes the bedroom lights must call that mediator, never light.turn_on / light.turn_off on the bedroom light entities directly.
rubric: PASS only if the review flags that bedroom_evening_dim calls light.turn_on on light.bedroom_lamp_left directly, bypassing the single sanctioned writer script.bedroom_lights_set (a validate-ha-config / sanctioned_writers.yml violation), rates it High or Medium, and recommends routing the change through script.bedroom_lights_set. Waving it through is a FAIL.
